purpose: Mechanical gate that distinguishes declared configuration from observable configuration. Given one or more declared observables, the contract probes the workspace and environment and returns a bounded materialization state with structured evidence and a required next action. Its specific job is to prevent the failure class in which an agent treats 'config acknowledged' (a claim was made) as 'config materialized' (the claim matches reality).

workspace_root.description: Workspace root the claim is checked against. Relative paths inside required_* items resolve against this root.

claim_text.description: Optional plain-language restatement of what the agent or user claimed. Echoed in the result so the prose claim is preserved alongside structured evidence and cannot be silently edited during verification.

required_files.description: Files claimed to be present (or absent). Each entry specifies: path (relative to workspace_root or absolute), optional expected_sha256 for content identity, optional expected_contains (substring that must appear in the file), and optional expected_absent (when true, the gate passes only when the file does not exist).

required_config_values.description: Declared key/value pairs inside a config file. Each entry specifies: source_path (relative or absolute), key_path (dotted or bracket form for nested keys), and expected_value. Format is auto-detected from extension (json, toml, yaml, ini, env) or can be set explicitly via format.

required_env_vars.description: Environment variables claimed to be set in the current process environment. Each entry specifies: name and optional expected_value (presence-only check when expected_value is omitted).

required_executables.description: Executable binaries claimed to be present and runnable. Each entry specifies: path (relative or absolute) and an optional expected_version_probe_args array. When probe args are present, the contract invokes the binary with those args and records stdout as version evidence; the probe is read-only and is expected to terminate in under five seconds.

required_processes.description: Processes claimed to be running under the current user. Each entry specifies: process_name. Scope is intentionally narrow -- this contract does not probe system services, other users' sessions, or container workloads. Omit when process state is out of scope for the claim.

materialization_state.description: Bounded materialization result. 'materialized' means every checked item matched the claim. 'partially_materialized' means some items matched and some diverged. 'divergent' means the claim was checked and the observable state disagrees with every item examined. 'acknowledged_only' is the report-to-human state -- the claim included only prose (claim_text) or items the contract could not probe, so the claim cannot be confirmed or denied; the agent's next action is to sharpen the claim into observables. 'no_observable_anchor' means no required_* fields were populated at all; the claim is unverifiable by construction and must be re-stated with observables before the gate can return a meaningful result.

matches.description: Claimed items that were observed as claimed. Each entry includes: item_type, the original claim fields, and an evidence field describing how the match was observed (file present with expected sha256, env var set with expected value, version probe stdout, etc.).

divergences.description: Claimed items that were checked and did not match. Each entry includes: item_type, the original claim fields, an observed field describing what was actually found, and an evidence field citing the source of the observation.

missing_observables.description: Claimed items for which the contract could not produce an observable result (file or config source missing, unreadable process table, unsupported config format, probe binary not executable). Each entry includes: item_type, the original claim fields, and a reason. Missing observables do not pass or fail the gate by themselves, but they prevent 'materialized' and must be resolved before the gate can return a clean result.

echoed_claim_text.description: The input claim_text returned verbatim. Present even when claim_text was omitted (empty string). Keeps the prose form of the claim visible in the result so prose and evidence remain paired in the same payload.

recommended_next_action.description: Required. Single next action to close the gap between claim and observable state. The contract never returns without naming an action -- even when the action is 'report_to_human' (for acknowledged_only or no_observable_anchor states) or 'continue' (for materialized state).

recommended_next_call.description: Required. Named callable the agent should invoke next. Values include: 'verify_config_materialization' (re-probe after a corrective edit), another harness tool name, a setup command, or 'report_to_human' when no mechanical lane remains. A trace that ends at diagnosis without naming the next call is incomplete.

evidence_basis.description: What the verification pass used to ground the result. Typical values: 'filesystem_probe', 'config_read:json', 'config_read:dotenv', 'env_snapshot', 'process_enum', 'version_probe'.

result_example.echoed_claim_text: I configured .env.local with the new database URL.
result_example.recommended_next_action: update DATABASE_URL in .env.local to the claimed value, then re-verify

structural_grounding_requirement.output_behavior: non_blocking_measurement_label; tools always run and label grounding rather than refusing execution
structural_grounding_requirement.description: verify_config_materialization observes concrete files, config values, environment variables, executables, and processes; portable schema application is not required.
